topic: Free will is an illusion; every choice you make is predetermined by physics.
Is it more important to maximize happiness or to minimize suffering?
Morality is entirely socially constructed and objective right or wrong does not exist.
In the Ship of Theseus paradox, if we replace every single cell in a human body over 7 years, is it still the exact same person?
Roko's Basilisk is just a techno-futurist reinvention of Pascal's Wager for people who read too much sci-fi.
Maxwell's Demon suggests that information is intrinsically linked to entropy. Does this mean information is a fundamental physical property of the universe?
The Trolley Problem is a useless philosophical toy that has absolutely no practical application in programming real-world AI morality.
